title: Gene expression ATLAS of Arabidopsis thaliana (accession Columbia) across its lifecycle
author: Jaiswal, Pankaj
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Abstract:  Arabidopsis thaliana (accession- Columbia) is an important model plant. RNA-Seq based study of 36 gene expression libraries was carried out to explore transcriptional programs operating in different plant parts (seedling, rosette, root, inflorescence, flower, fruit silique, and seed) and developmental stages (2-leaf stage, 6-leaf stage, 12-leaf stage, senescence stage, dry mature and imbibed seed stage). For each tissue type and developmental stage, three individual plants were used as biological replicates. 
   Organism part:    inflorescence, whole plant, seed, root, silique fruit, flower, rosette  
   
   Developmental stage:    LP.02 two leaves visible stage, IL.00 inflorescence just visible stage, fruit size 30 to 50% stage, LP.12 twelve leaves visible stage, root development stage, fruit size 70% to final stage, LP.06 six leaves visible stage, dry seed stage, flowering stage, seed imbibition stage, sporophyte senescent stage, inflorescence development stage  
   
 
   Organism:    Arabidopsis thaliana  
   
   Ecotype:    Col-0  
   
   Genotype:    wild type genotype  
   
   Age:  Samples are from   20-day, 49-day, 39-day, 15-day, 21-day, 9-day, 22-day, 55-day, 26-day, 45-day  
   
    Experimental Designs:    growth chamber study  EFO  ,  development or differentiation design  EFO  ,  organism part comparison design  EFO    
   
  For more description of the data and sample types see the file  PRJEB24664_Sample_descriptors.xlsx or visit    or visit  https://www.ebi.ac.uk/biostudies/arrayexpress/studies/E-MTAB-6422/sdrf   
   
  Original data was submitted from   
 
 
  EMBL-EBI ArraExpress:  https://www.ebi.ac.uk/biostudies/arrayexpress/studies/E-MTAB-6422   
  NCBI SRA:  https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/bioproject/PRJEB24664   
 
   Protocol description:   
 

  
 Name 
 Type 
 Description 
 Hardware 
 

  
 
 P-MTAB-71349 
  growth protocol  EFO   
 Seeds were planted in pots containing commercial potting mix with fertilizers. Pots were covered with clear perforated plastic wrap and kept at 4 degrees celsius for 3 days to break the dormancy. After 3 days plants were transferred to the Intellus Ultra growth chamber (Percival Scientific, IA, USA) which was set to temperature 22-23 degrees celsius, light intensity 120-150 micromol/m2sec under the cycle of 16h light and 8h dark. Soil was kept moist by gently spraying with water every 72 hours to maintain humidity to 50-60%. Sampling time point is given in days after germination. 
   
 
 
 P-MTAB-71350 
  nucleic acid extraction protocol  EFO   
 Total RNA from frozen samples was extracted as a method described in Filichkin et al., 2010. Total RNA was used to isolate large RNA as per manufacturer's protocol for miRNeasy Mini kits (Qiagen Inc., USA), and RNase-free DNase (Life Technologies Inc., USA). 
   
 
 
 P-MTAB-71351 
  nucleic acid library construction protocol  EFO   
 True-Seq kit (Illumina Inc.) was used to prepare RNA-seq libraries, according to the manufacturer’s protocol. 
   
 
 
 P-MTAB-71352 
  nucleic acid sequencing protocol  EFO   
 101bp paired-end sequencing of mRNA was performed by using the standard protocols on Illumina HiSeq 3000. 
 Illumina HiSeq 3000
